{"step_by_step_thinking": "Herpangina is a viral infection that causes ulcers in the back of the throat and is relatively common in children. Dental cysts are benign growths that can occur in the jaw and are not uncommon in children. Cherubism is a rare genetic disorder that causes benign tumors to grow in the jaws, affecting the appearance of the face. Smallpox, while eradicated, was a highly contagious and deadly disease that is extremely rare today. Given these descriptions, smallpox is the rarest option.", "answer_choice": "D"}
